response: We propose a new method for [task/domain], evaluating its performance against a strong baseline on the standard test set. Across five random seeds, our method achieves an average accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1%, compared to 72.2 ± 0.9% for the baseline—a gain of +1.2 percentage points. A two-sample t-test yields t = 1.04 and p = 0.31, with a 95% confidence interval for the improvement of [–1.1, +3.5], indicating that the observed difference is not statistically significant. These results suggest that the proposed method performs at least as well as a strong baseline on this benchmark, but further experiments with larger sample sizes are needed to confirm any real advantage. This work provides a promising direction for future research, while highlighting the importance of rigorous statistical assessment in benchmarking new methods.
